description_long: This is a headless stone sculpture of a robed figure standing on a rectangular pedestal. The figure holds a detailed, bearded head in its left hand and a chalice or goblet in its right hand, suggesting a symbolic or religious theme. The robe is intricately carved with visible folds and drapery, and the base is inscribed with the words 'HEILIGE LOSBOL.' The sculpture has a weathered, reddish-brown patina, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The pedestal is made of a different, smoother stone, likely granite or concrete, and features a plaque on one side. The overall form is solid and monumental, with no visible moving parts.
description: A weathered, headless stone statue of a robed figure holding a bearded head and a chalice, mounted on a rectangular pedestal inscribed 'HEILIGE LOSBOL.' The sculpture has detailed drapery and a reddish-brown patina.
description_view_0: Front view showing the headless robed figure holding a bearded head in the left hand and a chalice in the right, with the 'HEILIGE LOSBOL' inscription clearly visible on the pedestal.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the extended right arm with the chalice and the side profile of the robe's folds; the plaque on the pedestal is also visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the robe with its drapery and the solid, unadorned back of the pedestal.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the left arm holding the bearded head and the side drapery of the robe, with less detail visible on the pedestal.
attribute_subject_matter: headless robed human figure holding a bearded head and a chalice
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is mounted on a stone pedestal, suggesting an outdoor or public urban setting such as a plaza or park.
